MainContent.dictDef1: She is this house. She is the table. She is the dinner that never repeats.
MainContent.dictDef2: She is the feeling that you are exactly where you need to be.

HeritageSection.heading: Where cultural heritage meets culinary excellence
HeritageSection.description: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

CoursesSection.label: COURSES
CoursesSection.desc1: Dinner is a 9-course seasonal affair, with produce from the region and the people we know.
CoursesSection.desc2: The menu changes with every season — following the rhythm of nature, not of habit.
CoursesSection.desc3: You can add a carefully selected wine pairing or a non-alcoholic pairing of ferments and seasonal drinks.
CoursesSection.desc4: Optionally — morning begins slowly, with a 5-course breakfast served at ease, without a clock.

TableSection.label: TABLE
TableSection.desc1: There are no separate tables here. There is one large table with twelve seats.
TableSection.desc2: Everyone sits together. No roles. No rush. Strangers become companions, and dinner becomes a shared experience.
TableSection.desc3: The table gathers people who come not just to eat, but to feel the rhythm of the place.

StorySection.farAway1: From the noise. From the rush. From places that all look the same.
StorySection.farAway2: Where seasons can still be felt, and silence carries meaning.
StorySection.farAway3: Somewhere among the hills of the Balkans hides a small village that you don't find by accident.
StorySection.house2: Inside, wondrous things happen. Slowly. Quietly. With great care.
StorySection.house3: The fire crackles. The seasons set the rhythm. And the table gathers people who come not just to eat, but to experience.
StorySection.whatIs1: ONÀ was born right here — as an idea for a kitchen that does not rush, does not repeat itself, and does not try to be everything for everyone.
StorySection.whatIs2: A kitchen that follows the season. The place. The mood of the village.
StorySection.whatIs3: Our menu changes. The evenings are full of stories. And the experience is always a little… wondrous.
StorySection.night1: We have two restored village houses for overnight stays.
StorySection.night2: The stay is a compliment to the dinner — so there is no rush, and the night remains part of the experience.

Footer.scheduleTueFri: On the countryside we work every day.

AboutPage.storyHeading: A place where every dish tells a story
AboutPage.storyText1: ONA was born from a deep love for Bulgarian culinary traditions and a desire to share them with the world. Nestled in the heart of Skatevtsi, our restaurant is a celebration of heritage, flavor, and artistry.
AboutPage.storyText2: Every ingredient we use is carefully sourced from local farms and producers, ensuring that each plate reflects the authenticity and richness of our land.
AboutPage.philosophyHeading: Rooted in tradition, refined by passion
AboutPage.philosophyText: We believe that great food begins with great respect — for the ingredients, the process, and the people who gather around the table. Our kitchen is guided by seasonality, simplicity, and a relentless pursuit of excellence.
AboutPage.value1Text: Every ingredient tells a story of the land it comes from. We partner with local farmers and artisans.
AboutPage.value2Text: Each dish is a work of art, meticulously prepared with techniques passed down through generations.
AboutPage.value3Text: We create an atmosphere where every guest feels like family, welcomed with warmth and care.
AboutPage.teamText: Our team brings together years of culinary expertise, a shared passion for Bulgarian cuisine, and an unwavering commitment to creating unforgettable dining experiences.
AboutPage.filipBio:
My name is Filip Zahariev.

I've been a dreamer for as long as I can remember. For years, I dreamed about having a small, cozy restaurant of my own. At the beginning, if I'm honest, it wasn't for the right reasons. Part of me just didn't want a boss. I didn't want to explain myself to anyone. I thought that because I could cook well, I was ready.

I wasn't. And realizing that probably saved me.

I started cooking when I was six. Nothing fancy, just summer squash with oil, garlic, dill, and salt, baked in the oven while my parents weren't home. Would be better if I add the dill in the end, but I was 6.

At eighteen, I chose the kitchen over everything else. I started in my hometown, Vidin, then moved to Sofia. For a short time, I even tried studying law, but I quickly understood that my place wasn't there. I went back to the kitchen and never left.

The real learning started when I left Bulgaria. Finland, Norway, long hours, hard kitchens, difficult chefs. The kind of places that break you first and then slowly build you again. Later, I worked at the one Michelin starred Onda in Oslo, where I refined my craft, but at the same time understood, that the Michelin star — it doesn't really change anything for the kitchen team. It doesn't really mean a thing.

The place that shaped me the most was Gruvelageret in Svalbard.

There, in this small, northernmost town on Earth, I became a head chef. For six years I wasn't just cooking, I was part of building and running a restaurant in one of the most extreme places in the world. No shortcuts, no excuses. Just work, responsibility, and learning from your own mistakes every single day.

That's where I understood that I was finally ready.

In 2019, I came back to Bulgaria and bought a few old houses in a small village in the northwest. No infrastructure, no guarantees, no real reason to believe it would work. It took two years of rebuilding, mistakes, starting over, and doing things again when they didn't feel right the first time.

I put everything into it — my savings, loans, time, and a lot of stubbornness.

ONÀ is the result.

It's not perfect. It was never meant to be. But it's honest. It's warm. It's exactly the kind of place I was dreaming about when I was younger — just built the hard way, not the naive one.
AboutPage.plamBio:
Plamena is the person who will greet you, pour you a glass of wine, and make sure the evening flows easily, naturally, and with good spirit.

A gourmand at heart, she has been through nearly every role in a restaurant — server, bartender, deliveries, even an internship in the kitchen.

Her last stop before ONÀ was The Mark Restaurant by Jean‑Georges in Manhattan, where she worked as manager. Before that she mixed cocktails behind the bar at Sputnik Cocktail Bar and Cosmos Restaurant — places that shaped her sense of taste, hospitality, and atmosphere.

Plamena loves wine. She doesn't call herself a sommelier — in her view there are at least another one to two thousand bottles that need to be opened before she allows herself that title. She holds WSET Level 2 for wine and continues to learn, mostly through tastings and travel.

She is also the most honest taster of the food in the house — critical, direct, and always with an opinion. She travels a lot and almost always for the same reason: food, flavors, and people.

Plamena is an extrovert who genuinely loves people. Her role at ONÀ is simple — to make guests feel comfortable, at ease, and a little further from the everyday.

And when she's not answering emails — she kneads. She kneads a lot. Every type of bread in the restaurant is her doing.

By education she's in advertising. But the moment she steps behind the bar, everything else somehow ends. And so begins the path that ultimately leads to ONÀ.
AboutPage.hrisoBio:
Almost a year ago, new energy entered the veins of the restaurant. Hristian is the engineer at the heart of the restaurant — the kitchen. He discovered his passion for cooking at an early age, passed down by his grandmother, somewhere in the distant year of 2003. Those bold enough to travel the road to the village of Stakevtsi will find out how and why.

He first started working in a kitchen in the summer of 2016, still a student, just for the summer and seemingly in jest — but not quite. The next year the scenario repeated, this time on the Bulgarian Black Sea coast. In 2018 he interrupted his university education in Bulgaria, and in early 2019 left for Malta, where he enrolled in a Bachelor in Culinary Arts at the island nation's tourism institute. On his way to fulfilling his dream, COVID arrived — at the very same time he first stepped into a Michelin kitchen: The Harbour Club, recognized in the first edition of the eponymous guide for the island.

A year later he departed as a scholarship student to France, to the Institute Paul Bocuse academy, where he completed a course in International Management in Culinary Art while simultaneously interning in the kitchen of the future champion of the Bocuse d'Or 2021, restaurant Saisons — Ecully, distinguished with one Michelin star. Upon returning to Malta, Hristian took on the role of junior sous chef at restaurant Fernando Gastrotheque. He spent nearly two years in the kitchen of this small restaurant, but together with its head chef they never stopped dreaming of the star — and it did not delay. In this restaurant Hristian became part of the team that earned the distinction every chef dreams of.

2022 presented an opportunity not to be taken lightly, and with a bit of luck and persistence he left for the United States, Chicago, where he became part of the team at a restaurant with over a quarter century of history, holder of three Michelin stars through early 2026 — Alinea, under head chef Grant Achatz.

The end of this thrilling adventure opened the door for him to return to Bulgaria, where he became culinary director of restaurant Cosmos in 2023, on the eve of Bulgaria's addition to JRE. Hristian actively participated in creating the restaurant's book alongside his colleagues, used his time there to develop his passion for wine, and together with Plamena completed WSET Level 2. The imprint left by the pandemic allowed him to enroll in a master's program in Spain, at Nebrija University, in the field of Gastronomy Innovations — once again shifting the winds for Hristian.

Early 2025 — the suitcase is packed again, the destination once more is the USA, but not just anywhere: the Big Apple, New York City, to join the team of yet another restaurant that has held the title of Best Restaurant in the World, hanging on its wall for 14 years the coveted red plaque with three stars and until recently the only vegan restaurant with that distinction — Eleven Madison Park. And so it goes until August of that same year, when Hristian together with Plamena and ONA together with ON, find themselves in the village of Stakevtsi to knock you off your feet.

PrivacyPage.metaDescription: How ONA collects, uses, and protects your personal data in accordance with GDPR.
PrivacyPage.s1p1: Welcome to ONA. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
PrivacyPage.s1p2: This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information when you visit our website or make a reservation.
PrivacyPage.s2p1: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:
PrivacyPage.s3intro: We may collect and process the following data:
PrivacyPage.s3personal: Personal Information: Name, email address, phone number (e.g., when making a reservation or contacting us)
PrivacyPage.s3usage: Usage Data: IP address, browser type, pages visited, and time spent on the website
PrivacyPage.s3cookies: Cookies and Tracking Data: Collected through cookies and similar technologies
PrivacyPage.s3analytics: Analytics Data: Collected via Google Analytics to understand website usage
PrivacyPage.s4intro: We process your personal data based on:
PrivacyPage.s4legitimate: Legitimate interests (e.g., improving our services and website)
PrivacyPage.s4consent: Consent (e.g., for cookies and analytics where required)
PrivacyPage.s5i2: Communicate with you regarding your booking
PrivacyPage.s6p1: We do not sell your personal data. We may share your data with:
PrivacyPage.s6li1: Service providers, such as Google Analytics
PrivacyPage.s6li2: Legal authorities, when required by law
PrivacyPage.s6p3: Google Analytics may process your data outside the EU. We ensure appropriate safeguards are in place.
PrivacyPage.s7p1: We use cookies to improve your browsing experience and analyze website traffic.
PrivacyPage.s7p2: You can manage or disable cookies through your browser settings. Where required, we will request your consent before placing non-essential cookies.
PrivacyPage.s8intro: We retain personal data only as long as necessary:
PrivacyPage.s8li1: Reservation data: retained for operational and legal purposes
PrivacyPage.s8li2: Analytics data: retained according to Google Analytics settings
PrivacyPage.s9intro: If you are located in the EU, you have the right to:
PrivacyPage.s9i5: Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority
PrivacyPage.s10p1: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data from unauthorized access, loss, or misuse.
PrivacyPage.s11p1: Our website may contain links to external websites. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.
PrivacyPage.s12p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page.
PrivacyPage.s13p1: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or your data, please contact us:

articles.title: Seasonal Menu Launch
articles.excerpt: Discover our new spring menu featuring the finest local ingredients and innovative flavor combinations.

articles.title: Wine Tasting Evening
articles.excerpt: Join us for an exclusive evening of Bulgarian wines paired with specially curated dishes from our chef.

articles.title: Chef's Table Experience
articles.excerpt: An intimate dining experience where our head chef prepares a bespoke multi-course meal right before your eyes.

articles.title: Farm-to-Table Partnership
articles.excerpt: We are proud to announce our new partnership with local organic farms in the Rhodope mountains.

articles.title: Holiday Special Menu
articles.excerpt: Celebrate the festive season with our specially crafted holiday menu featuring traditional Bulgarian delicacies.

articles.title: Culinary Workshop
articles.excerpt: Learn the secrets of Bulgarian cuisine in our hands-on workshop led by our award-winning chef.

BookPage.introText: Join us for an unforgettable dining experience. Reserve your table and let us take care of the rest.
BookPage.info1Text: Smart casual. We want you to feel comfortable while enjoying the ambiance.
BookPage.info2Text: Please notify us at least 2 weeks in advance if you need to cancel or modify your reservation.
BookPage.info3Text: For groups of 8 or more, please contact us directly by phone for special arrangements.
BookPage.detailsIntro1: Evenings at ONÀ are small, carefully prepared, and happen only for a limited number of guests.
BookPage.detailsIntro2: Each evening is different, tailored to the season, the produce, and the mood of the place.
BookPage.dinner1: Dinner at ONÀ starts at 8:00 PM.
BookPage.dinner2: All guests sit together around one shared table. There are no separate tables — conversation and shared time are part of the experience.
BookPage.dinner3: The menu is a 7-course seasonal affair inspired by the local nature. The dishes change constantly based on the produce we find in the region.
BookPage.drinksWine: 5-course wine pairing — carefully selected to complement each dish. We highly recommend this option.
BookPage.drinksNonAlc: 5-course non-alcoholic pairing — a selection of kombuchas, ferments, and seasonal mixed drinks.
BookPage.detailsSmallTitle: Small details, part of the experience
BookPage.detailsSlippers: Upon entering, you put on Bulgarian wool slippers — for comfort, warmth, and the slow rhythm of the evening.
BookPage.detailsChildren: Children over 7 are welcome. The menu and price are the same as for adults.
BookPage.detailsPets: Pets are also welcome, if they can stay in the room during dinner.
BookPage.stay1: The overnight stay is in restored village houses and is included in the experience.
BookPage.stay2: Bathrooms are shared between two rooms — part of the authentic character of the place.
BookPage.stay3: The following morning we offer a 5-course tasting breakfast, served slowly and at ease. (not included in the price)
BookPage.stay4: Alternatively, you can have breakfast at Han Madonna, about 25 minutes from Stakevtsi.
BookPage.res1: Reservations are accepted for one evening.
BookPage.res2: Arrival: 6:00 PM. Dinner starts: 8:00 PM. Room checkout: around 11:00 AM the next day.
BookPage.res3: Full prepayment is required to confirm the reservation.
BookPage.res4: The amount is non-refundable in case of no-show or cancellation.
BookPage.res5: With at least 14 days' notice, the date can be changed subject to availability.
BookPage.note1: The village only has coverage from Vivacom and Yettel.
BookPage.note2: There is no Wi-Fi. And that's part of the experience 🙂
BookPage.detailsContact: For full terms, payment details, and additional information, you can reach us by email or through the form below.

VoucherPage.introHeading: Sometimes the most beautiful gift is not an object, but a memory.
VoucherPage.introText: The ONA voucher is an invitation to an evening that lingers long after the candles go out.
VoucherPage.scrollText: A dark-green leaf with the ONA logo — the first trace towards an evening far from almost everything.
VoucherPage.blankText: A finely illustrated sheet, left intentionally blank. For words written by hand.
VoucherPage.blankNote: Because there is no more beautiful gesture than a personal message — with thought, with care, with love.
VoucherPage.howText1: Evenings at ONA are small and carefully prepared. That is why our vouchers are tied to a specific date.
VoucherPage.howText2: We do not work with open reservations — each evening is created specially for a particular moment and a set number of guests.
VoucherPage.howText3: If the chosen date is not convenient for the recipient, it can be changed up to 14 days in advance, so we have time to invite another guest to the freed seat.
VoucherPage.howNote: In case of no-show or cancellation, the voucher amount is non-refundable.
VoucherPage.exp1: Dinner at ONA is a 7-course seasonal menu inspired by the local nature and produce around us.
VoucherPage.exp2: All guests share one common table — conversation is part of the evening.
VoucherPage.exp3: The overnight stay is a gift from us — in renovated village houses in Stakevtsi.
VoucherPage.exp4: Bathrooms are shared between two rooms, part of the authentic character and simple comfort of the place.
VoucherPage.addon1: Wine pairing — carefully selected for each dish
VoucherPage.addon2: Non-alcoholic pairing — ferments, kombuchas and seasonal drinks
VoucherPage.addon3: 5-course breakfast, served at ease — without a clock, without rush.
VoucherPage.ctaText: If you wish to gift an experience at ONA, we will gladly send you the full terms and payment information. You can reach us via the form below or by email.
VoucherPage.ctaClosing: Sometimes the most beautiful gifts are those experienced together.
